At least one of the reveal sequences anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:47, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:271, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:393 must force a distinct changed condition that is still visible by snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:603, not merely another unsettled silence.

[chapter_02_f02_miriam_recognition_convenience] Pass if, by the time Miriam names Ganymede at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:397, the revised scene has already established a concrete reason she is plausibly here now and a concrete reason the Golden Ox memory would have stayed with her. Fail if the beat still depends mainly on the reader accepting coincidence plus a brief 'I rotate' after the fact.

A reader should be able to point to a specific leverage change in the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:69, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:303, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:441 that disrupts the template instead of restating it.

After the first involuntary touch at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:429, there should be no more than one additional collarbone-touch beat in the rest of the chapter, and the later stress beats must still read clearly without reusing the same gesture.

[chapter_02_f05_clipped_fragment_chains] Pass if the cited passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:21, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:93, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:277, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:387, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:541 no longer contain narration built from subject-less fragment chains such as 'Not X. Just Y.' Each revised sentence in those spans must read as grammatically complete prose, not telegraphic shorthand.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter no longer ends all three major movements in variants of private withholding. At least one of the reveal sequences anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:47, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:271, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:393 must force a distinct changed condition that is still visible by snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:603, not merely another unsettled silence.
findings.problem: The chapter parcels its three personalized discoveries into a visibly tidy sequence and resolves each with the same basic outcome: a reveal lands, the implicated character denies or minimizes it, and the scene settles into private unease. That symmetry keeps the chapter legible, but at award level it also makes the discovery engine feel outlined rather than emergent.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise at least one of the three reveal blocks so it produces a qualitatively different on-page consequence instead of another withheld beat. The cleanest targets are the Jet block around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:47 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:139, the Spike block around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:271 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:303, and the regroup around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:565 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:603. Give one scene a new pressure type on-page: a procedural consequence, a relational rupture, or a concrete lead that changes what the next hour on the Bebop must be.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, by the time Miriam names Ganymede at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:397, the revised scene has already established a concrete reason she is plausibly here now and a concrete reason the Golden Ox memory would have stayed with her. Fail if the beat still depends mainly on the reader accepting coincidence plus a brief 'I rotate' after the fact.
findings.problem: Faye's destabilizing clue arrives through a chance recognition that is only thinly grounded on-page. Miriam happens to be covering this shift, happens to notice Faye, and happens to carry a highly consequential memory from a different world; the chapter offers only a late 'I rotate' explanation. That keeps the beat readable, but it still reads as a plot handout more than an earned discovery.
findings.rewrite_direction: Strengthen the causal bridge inside the noodle-shop scene. Seed why Miriam is at Mama Yun's before the recognition turns plot-critical and why this memory stayed unusually vivid. Target the setup around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:355 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:357, then sharpen the recognition exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:393, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:405, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:485 so the reader understands why this witness, on this shift, can make this identification now.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if at least one of the cited exchanges no longer follows a claim -> denial/minimization -> 'that is not an answer' style loop when read straight through. A reader should be able to point to a specific leverage change in the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:69, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:303, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:441 that disrupts the template instead of restating it.
findings.problem: The chapter's major conversations reuse the same denial ladder often enough to feel composed rather than overheard: a claim is made, the challenged character denies or shrinks scope, the other speaker points out that this is not an answer, and the exchange stalls into withholding. The voices remain distinct, but the repeated turn-shape gives the chapter a generated conversational skeleton.
findings.rewrite_direction: Break the repeated ladder in at least one major exchange. The likeliest targets are Jet's evasion around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:69 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:149, Spike's mutual concealment exchange around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:303 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:313, and Faye/Miriam's insistence-denial loop around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:441 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:455. Replace one balanced deny-and-counter pattern with messier human pressure: interruption, abandonment, topic slippage, a concrete task cutting the talk short, or a speaker saying the wrong thing rather than the neat withholding thing.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter uses the collarbone gesture as a distinct reveal rather than a recurring stamp. After the first involuntary touch at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:429, there should be no more than one additional collarbone-touch beat in the rest of the chapter, and the later stress beats must still read clearly without reusing the same gesture.
findings.problem: Faye's collarbone touch is a good tell the first time, but the chapter repeats it so often that the gesture starts reading as a style-bible checkbox instead of involuntary behavior. Because the clue is central, the repetition is noticeable fast; by the return-to-Bebop beat it has flattened into a mechanical callback.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the first involuntary collarbone touch as the scene's decisive proof point, then vary the later manifestations of stress instead of replaying the same gesture. The key targets are snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:427, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:469, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:497, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:535, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:605. After the initial trigger lands, translate later pressure into different physical behavior, sharper evasive dialogue, or altered handling of the noodles and cup.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the cited passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:21, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:93, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:277, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:387, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:541 no longer contain narration built from subject-less fragment chains such as 'Not X. Just Y.' Each revised sentence in those spans must read as grammatically complete prose, not telegraphic shorthand.
findings.problem: The narration repeatedly falls into clipped fragment chains with dropped subjects or auxiliaries: 'Not illegal. Not even hidden very well. Just old.'; 'Not dramatic. Not a double take.'; 'Not bigger or smaller. Watched, maybe.' That is not just a prose tic here; it directly violates the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration and gives the chapter a conspicuously manufactured rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the clipped fragment clusters into grammatically complete sentences while preserving cadence and close-third pressure. Start with the highest-visibility cases at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:21, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:93, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:99, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:277, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:387, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:541. The goal is not to make the prose flatter; it is to keep the syncopation without relying on subject-less shorthand.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise demands tonal range that most novels avoid: genuine slapstick comedy in the same book as genuine grief, syndicate violence alongside domestic warmth, existential identity crisis alongside arguments about noodles. The risk is not in the dark material — it's in the tonal juxtaposition. Commit to both registers fully. A comic scene should be actually funny, not wryly amusing. A grief scene should be actually painful, not tastefully sad. The Bebop register earns its power by refusing to choose between comedy and tragedy — they coexist in the same room, the same conversation, the same character. Do not smooth the transitions between registers. Let them be jarring when the material is jarring. Let a joke land in the middle of a painful conversation if that's what the character would do. Let a painful truth arrive during a comic scene without the comedy stopping to make room for it. The creative risk is trust: trust that the reader can hold both, trust that tonal instability is a form of emotional honesty, not a failure of control.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure. When a scene arrives at a moment of physical, emotional, or moral consequence, the prose must stay present — naming, rendering, inhabiting. Summary, vagueness, and decorative circumlocution at the point of impact are disallowed. If a character is hurt, drunk, broke, grieving, or afraid, render the specific texture of that experience. 'She was devastated' is not writing. 'She found his grocery list on the fridge' is writing. The Bebop world is broke, dangerous, and populated by people who have lost things they cannot get back. The prose must honor that.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific precision. The Bebop crew lives in contractions; full forms should feel conspicuous when they appear.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Consecutive chapters must differ in at least two of: scene count, dialogue density, pacing, primary emotional register, and structural shape. A dialogue-heavy chapter should be followed by one with more interiority or action. A multi-scene chapter should be followed by one with fewer scenes or a different temporal rhythm. Vary paragraph length distribution across chapters — some chapters should run long and flowing, others should be built from shorter, harder units. The novel's rhythm at the chapter level should feel like a jazz set: different tempos, different keys, with throughlines that connect them.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, vernacular-adjacent, unsentimental. The narration should sound like an extension of the characters' world — working-class, spacefaring, slightly retro-future. Avoid elevated literary diction unless the passage specifically calls for it (rare). Avoid clinical or academic language. The narrator is not above the characters; the narrator is in the room with them. Latinate vocabulary should be rare and purposeful. Anglo-Saxon roots preferred for physical description.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating by chapter or scene as indicated in the outline. The camera sits behind the POV character's eyes — we see what they see, hear their internal register in the narration, and do not access other characters' thoughts except through observed behavior. When the chapter is ensemble, the camera moves between characters within scenes but does not overlay multiple interiorities simultaneously. Maintain enough distance that the narrator's voice is present alongside the character's — this is not stream of consciousness but inhabited third person.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Jazz-inflected variation. Sentences should vary aggressively in length and structure — short declaratives next to longer compound sentences, fragments where they create rhythmic force (but never elliptical clipping). The Bebop register favors syncopation: beat expectations set up and then broken. Avoid metronomic alternation (short-long-short-long). Let passages breathe with their own rhythms. Action sequences should accelerate sentence pace. Reflective passages should slow and lengthen. Transitions between registers should be controlled, not abrupt.
